name: active listening
description: being fully present with another person — not just waiting for your turn to speak. the kind of listening that makes people feel genuinely heard.
howToPractice: in your next conversation, resist the urge to form a response until the other person has fully finished. notice what you hear when you're not preparing to reply.

name: holding space
description: creating the conditions where someone can be honest — including with themselves. not fixing, not advising, not rushing to resolution.
howToPractice: when someone shares something hard, try sitting with it for ten seconds before responding. notice the difference between silence that empties and silence that holds.

name: naming what's true
description: saying the thing in the room that everyone senses but no one has said. not to create drama — to reduce the energy spent pretending it isn't there.
howToPractice: in your next group, notice what's unsaid. try articulating it as a question: 'i'm noticing a certain hesitation — is that landing for anyone else?'

name: self-regulation
description: the ability to notice your own activation — physical, emotional, cognitive — and work with it rather than from it. the foundation of everything else.
howToPractice: when you feel a spike of reactivity, put one hand on your chest and take three slow breaths. not to suppress the feeling — to expand the space around it.

name: embodied presence
description: showing up in your body, not just your head. awareness of posture, breath, and physical sensation as live information about what's happening.
howToPractice: before a difficult conversation, take 60 seconds to feel your feet on the floor, your breath in your belly, and the weight of your body in the chair.

name: boundary sensing
description: knowing — in your body, not just your mind — where you end and someone else begins. essential for genuine generosity rather than habitual over-giving.
howToPractice: notice the physical sensation of a 'yes that costs you something' versus a 'yes that fills you up'. the body knows the difference before the mind does.

name: reflective practice
description: the habit of pausing after experiences to extract meaning — not rumination, but deliberate inquiry into what happened and what it taught you.
howToPractice: after any significant session or conversation, ask three questions: what happened? what surprised me? what would i do differently?

name: assumption spotting
description: noticing the invisible beliefs and expectations you're carrying into any situation — before they become the floor you don't know you're standing on.
howToPractice: before your next facilitated session or important conversation, write down three things you assume will happen. check afterwards how many were true.

name: creative risk
description: trying something before it's perfect — the willingness to be awkward, to iterate, to learn in public. most creative blocks are perfection in disguise.
howToPractice: make one small thing today that you won't show anyone. practice the motion without the audience. notice what relaxes.

name: play
description: the capacity to engage with something for its own sake — without needing it to produce anything. play is practice for not knowing the outcome.
howToPractice: spend 20 minutes doing something with no goal and no audience. doodle. move. make noise. notice when the urge to 'make it into something' arises.

name: design thinking
description: approaching problems through questions, prototypes, and iterations rather than predetermined solutions. starting with curiosity about what's actually needed.
howToPractice: take a current problem you're trying to solve. instead of generating solutions, spend 10 minutes generating questions. see which ones crack the frame.
